description: APOL1 kidney-risk panel (African-ancestry-contextualized, recessive): G1 = rs73885319 (S342G, risk G) + rs60910145 (I384M, risk G, in cis); G2 = rs71785313 (6-bp deletion p.Asn388_Tyr389del; off-chip on most arrays; gnomAD also labels this deletion rs1317778148); N264K modifier = rs73885316 (risk-attenuating allele A/Lys). High risk requires TWO risk alleles across G1/G2 (recessive). G1 dosage is counted from the rs73885319 tag SNP ONLY in total_risk_dosage; rs60910145 is read for transparency/QC but deliberately NOT summed, because the two G1 SNPs are in cis on one haplotype — summing both would double-count G1 and mis-call a single-G1 heterozygote (one risk allele = low risk) as two-allele high-risk. rs73885319 is a validated tag (the two G1 SNPs are in near-absolute LD), so an off-chip/no-call rs60910145 does NOT block a tag-based G1 call (David et al. 2018, Kidney Int Rep). But when rs60910145 IS typed and corroborates LESS G1 dosage than the tag (concordant_rsids veto on rs73885319), the G1 call is unconfirmed and becomes indeterminate rather than high-risk — the veto is directional (partner dosage < tag dosage only), because the common ThermoFisher rs60910145 assay primer spans the G2 deletion and INFLATES rs60910145 (mis-scores G1/G2 and G2/G2 as homozygous-variant), so a partner showing MORE dosage than the tag is the known artifact and must not suppress a real call (David et al. 2018, PMID 30596185). Directly observed two-risk-allele genotypes are reported with an ancestry/validation caveat when top global ancestry is not predominantly AFR; indeterminate partial-only genotypes remain suppressed outside the validated ancestry context. N264K present attenuates G2-associated risk. Common risk alleles, not ClinVar P/LP -> clinvar_significance=NULL. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §9 / roadmap #27.

ancestry_gate.note: APOL1 G1/G2 risk estimates are validated primarily in recent African-ancestry backgrounds. Top global ancestry does not prove local ancestry at APOL1, so this directly observed genotype is shown with extra validation caution when inferred African ancestry is absent or below 50%; confirm clinically before any medical action.

loci.label: G2 (6-bp deletion, p.Asn388_Tyr389del)
loci.alias_rsids_note: Merged dbSNP/gnomAD synonym records of the SAME APOL1 G2 6-bp deletion as rs71785313: rs143830837 (the dbSNP record cited by David et al. 2018) and rs1317778148 (gnomAD's label). Verified 2026-06-13 via Ensembl — rs71785313's synonyms array lists both, all sharing allele_string ATAATTATAA/ATAA (6-bp inframe deletion) at chr22:36,265,996-36,266,005 (GRCh38), ClinVar VCV000006081 / OMIM 603743.0002. read_genotypes reads the union and resolves the first typed match so a G2 stored under an alias is not under-called as off-chip (#262).

indel_polarity.vendor_id_convention: 23andMe / AncestryDNA encode indel markers with literal I/D tokens where D = the deletion (shorter) allele and I = the insertion/reference (longer) allele; the ingested-array parsers (parser_23andme.py / parser_ancestrydna.py _canonical_genotype) pass these tokens through unchanged, so the D token resolves to the G2 6-bp deletion (risk_allele 'D') and I to the reference (ref_allele 'I').
sources: dbSNP rs71785313 — 6-bp deletion (p.Asn388_Tyr389del), variant class = deletion
Genovese et al. 2010, Science (PMID 20647424) — APOL1 G2 is a 6-bp deletion kidney-risk allele

genotype_models.odds_ratio: Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis OR 10.5 (95% CI 6.0–18.4); hypertension-attributed end-stage kidney disease OR 7.3 (95% CI 5.6–9.5), versus the low-risk (0–1 risk allele) genotype
genotype_models.absolute_risk_context: These are relative odds for specific kidney diseases, not an absolute or lifetime risk. Even with two risk alleles, most people never develop kidney disease — penetrance is incomplete and usually requires a 'second hit' (an inflammatory/interferon state, certain infections). This is a risk genotype, not a diagnosis.

modifier.unassessed_caveat: The N264K modifier (rs73885316), which strongly attenuates G2-associated kidney risk, was not assessed on this array, so this high-risk estimate may be overstated.

reclassify.risk_classification: APOL1 risk attenuated by N264K modifier
reclassify.finding_text: You carry an APOL1 two-risk-allele genotype, but you also carry the N264K modifier (rs73885316), which strongly attenuates G2-associated kidney-disease risk — this reclassifies the genotype toward low risk (Nat Commun 2023). Discuss interpretation with a clinician or genetic counselor.

partial_disclosure.finding_text: You carry one APOL1 risk allele ({genotype}), but at least one other APOL1 variant was not typed on this array — in particular the G2 6-bp deletion (rs71785313), which is frequently off-chip. Because APOL1 kidney risk is recessive (two risk alleles are needed), your status cannot be determined from these data: it would be high-risk if you also carry an untyped G2 (or second G1) risk allele, and low-risk if you do not. This is not a low-risk result. Consider sequencing-based confirmation and discuss with a clinician or genetic counselor.
